title: The Sick Rose
content:
O rose, thou art sick!
    The invisible worm,
    That flies in the night,
    In the howling storm,

    Has found out thy bed
    Of crimson joy,
    And his dark secret love
    Does thy life destroy.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Blake delivers a powerful performance in "The Sick Rose"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
